title: Fragile subcommand short-circuit in fabric config parent command
description: fabric config short-circuits subcommands (dismiss-slot, onboard-reset) by strictly checking process.argv[3]. If invoked with a flag before the subcommand (fabric config --target ./project dismiss-slot), argv[3] is '--target' not the subcommand, so the short-circuit is bypassed and the parent run launches the interactive Clack panel (or logs an uninitialized-workspace error and exits) instead of letting the subcommand finish.
fix_direction: Check whether the subcommand name exists anywhere in process.argv (process.argv.includes) or query the parsed citty command context instead of hardcoding argv indexes.

title: Unhandled promise rejection in experimental HTTP server /events endpoint
description: The /events endpoint passes the async handler from createEventsHandler directly. When ensureWatcher fails (server started in a non-Fabric dir, resolveLedgerPaths throws), the returned promise rejects. The router does not wrap this async call in a catch, so the rejection is unhandled — the connection hangs indefinitely or Express crashes without sending HTTP 500/diagnostic.
fix_direction: Wrap the /events handler in try/catch or an Express async wrapper to send a standard HTTP 500 via sendUnknownError when watcher setup fails.

title: Inconsistent argument types (named vs positional) across store subcommands
description: fabric store add / create require alias and uuid as named flags (--alias foo --uuid 123), while remove/explain/bind/switch-write take them positionally (fabric store remove foo). This inconsistency forces developers to memorize arbitrary syntax differences for closely-related subcommands.
fix_direction: Standardize argument types across all store subcommands — make add/create accept alias (and uuid/remote) positionally or via unified flags matching remove/explain.

title: Silent exit code 0 when detaching/explaining non-existent store aliases
description: fabric store remove <alias> / explain <alias> on a non-existent alias prints an error but still exits with status 0, masking failures so shell scripts / CI cannot detect the command failed.
fix_direction: Set process.exitCode = 1 when the operation returns null (detached === null / explanation === null) before completing.

title: fabric store switch-write lacks mounting validation, allows invalid targets
description: fabric store switch-write <alias> sets the project's active write store directly without verifying the alias is registered/mounted globally, so typos or unmounted aliases are written to project config silently, only failing later during write-heavy agent operations.
fix_direction: Verify the alias is mounted in global config (storeList()/storeExplain()) before writing it to project config; otherwise warn or throw a descriptive error.

title: Hardcoded English strings in fabric status bypass localization
description: Unlike other commands using getProjectTranslator(), fabric status hardcodes all labels/fallbacks in English ('uid:', '(no global config)', ...), breaking translation consistency for zh-CN users.
fix_direction: Use getProjectTranslator(process.cwd()) and add translation keys for status labels and fallback strings.

title: Redundant watch paths in experimental HTTP server cache watcher
description: In createFabricHttpApp, chokidar watches both '.fabric/knowledge/**/*.md' and '.fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md'. The first glob already covers pending recursively, so the second is redundant and creates extra FS watch descriptors.
fix_direction: Remove '.fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md' from the chokidar watch array since it is already covered by the broader glob.
